channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: Juno takes temperature of Jupiter's fiery moon Io - Phys.org
item.description:
1. Juno takes temperature of Jupiter's fiery moon Io  Phys.org

  4. Jupiter's moon Io is the most volcanically active body in the solar system, with hundreds of active volcanoes that can shoot sulphur plumes more than 500 kilometres above its surface — a landscape so extreme that early Voyager scientists initially mistook the p  Space Daily
  5. Nasa's Juno mission uncovered a surprising secret beneath Jupiter's volcanic moon Io  The Times of India

item.title: How Fast Is the Universe Really Expanding? - Quanta Magazine
item.description:
1. How Fast Is the Universe Really Expanding?  Quanta Magazine
  2. The observable universe is roughly 93 billion light-years across despite being only 13.8 billion years old, because space kept expanding while the oldest light was travelling toward us  Space Daily
  3. The Universe Can Expand Faster Than Light Without Breaking Physics  SciTechDaily

item.title: How to see the Perseids meteor shower this summer - ABC News - Breaking News, Latest News and Videos
item.description:
1. How to see the Perseids meteor shower this summer  ABC News - Breaking News, Latest News and Videos
  2. The Southern Delta Aquariids peak around July 30, but a nearly full Moon drowns all but the brightest meteors — so the dark mornings the week before are the ones to watch  Space Daily
  3. Best nights to watch July meteor showers in Mississippi  The Clarion-Ledger
  4. Perseid Meteor Shower Peaks August 12 And 13 With No Moonlight To Dim The Show  WROK 1440 AM / 96.1 FM
  5. The Perseid meteor shower has begun. It could be the best we've seen in years!  The Weather Network
item.source: ABC News - Breaking News, Latest News and Videos

item.title: NASA astronaut shares image of impossible-looking 'hopper' crystal growing on the space station. What is happening? - Live Science
item.description:
1. NASA astronaut shares image of impossible-looking 'hopper' crystal growing on the space station. What is happening?  Live Science
  2. A Crystal Almost Impossible to Grow on Earth Has Been Cultivated on the ISS. It's All About Microgravity  Inbox.eu

item.title: A glimpse of home was among the last images Voyager 1’s cameras ever sent. Just 34 minutes after taking the most distant photo of Earth ever, the cameras were powered off to conserve energy — going dark for good - Space Daily
item.description:
1. A glimpse of home was among the last images Voyager 1’s cameras ever sent. Just 34 minutes after taking the most distant photo of Earth ever, the cameras were powered off to conserve energy — going dark for good  Space Daily
  2. Voyager 1 on track to reach one light-day from Earth  it-online.co.za
  3. NASA's Voyager 1 Radio Transmitter Is Weaker Than a Refrigerator Lightbulb. It Still Reaches Earth From 15 Billion Miles Away  19FortyFive
  4. Voyager 1 is still transmitting from more than 25 billion kilometres away, powered by less than 250 watts of decaying plutonium — a signal that now takes over 23 hours to reach Earth, carried by a radio no stronger than a refrigerator lightbulb  Make Tech Easier
  5. NASA has calculated, to the precise second, when Voyager 1 will be exactly one light-day from Earth: 2:16 a.m. on 18 November 2026, at a distance of 16,094,799,096 miles — a number that would have been science fiction when the probe launched in 1977  Space Daily

  4. Fires raged for thousands of years during ancient global warming  The Conway Daily Sun
  5. Fires raged across Europe during “extreme global warming” 201 million years ago  IslanderNews.com

  5. Giant pit discovered by hiker on Google Maps confirmed to be 390 million-year-old meteor impact crater  Live Science

item.title: Cats sleep between 12 and 16 hours a day not because they are lazy but because their hunting strategy requires explosive short-burst energy — and a body that can sprint at nearly 50 kilometres per hour needs most of the day to prepare for a few seconds of - Space Daily

item.title: After nearly 30 years, NASA realized this near-Earth asteroid is actually a comet - Space
item.description:
1. After nearly 30 years, NASA realized this near-Earth asteroid is actually a comet  Space
  2. NASA Study Finds Near-Earth Asteroid Is Actually Comet  NASA (.gov)
  3. A near-Earth asteroid tracked since 1998 turned up far from its predicted spot in the sky, and astronomers have now confirmed why — it is secretly a comet, nudged off its path by escaping gas, with a tail too faint for ordinary telescopes to see  Space Daily
  4. Near-Earth asteroid not an asteroid at all  Popular Science
  5. Astronomers Catch a "Dark Comet" Red-Handed  Universe Today

item.title: Ancient collision with the Gaia Sausage dwarf galaxy 10 billion years ago may have flipped the Milky Way by more than 90 degrees, astronomers say - The Times of India

  4. Cosmic gymnastics: How our Milky Way once underwent a dramatic flip  EurekAlert!

item.title: Science thought the human lifespan was 122 years. A new model says we could live decades longer. - Yahoo News Canada
item.description:
1. Science thought the human lifespan was 122 years. A new model says we could live decades longer.  Yahoo News Canada

  3. Ultimate Limit of The Human Lifespan May Be Identified, Study Suggests  ScienceAlert
  4. Your Liver Could Live For Thousands Of Years Longer Than You: New Study Finds The Maximum Possible Lifespans For Humans  IFLScience
  5. Scientists May Have Found the Ultimate Limit of the Human Lifespan  VICE

item.title: Every winter, a single beluga whale in the Arctic will dive to 700 metres and stay under for 25 minutes on a lungful of air compressed to 1/70th its surface volume, sustained by muscles so oxygen-rich they run nearly black when cut - Space Daily

item.title: How does a planet 4.5 billion kilometres from the Sun, where daylight is roughly 900 times dimmer than on Earth, whip up the fastest winds in the entire solar system? - Space Daily
item.description:
1. How does a planet 4.5 billion kilometres from the Sun, where daylight is roughly 900 times dimmer than on Earth, whip up the fastest winds in the entire solar system?  Space Daily
  2. Neptune has the strongest winds in the solar system, gusting past 1,200 miles an hour on a planet that gets barely a fraction of the sunlight Earth does  Space Daily

item.title: A black bear's heart drops from roughly 55 beats per minute to as few as 8 during hibernation, yet it emerges in spring with almost no muscle or bone loss: a biological trick space agencies are studying to protect astronauts on long missions - Space Daily
item.description:
1. A black bear's heart drops from roughly 55 beats per minute to as few as 8 during hibernation, yet it emerges in spring with almost no muscle or bone loss: a biological trick space agencies are studying to protect astronauts on long missions  Space Daily

  3. NASA Is Not Preparing to Put Astronauts Into Hibernation for Mars Missions  srilanka.factcrescendo.com
  4. Nasa explored putting astronauts into a hibernation-like state for Mars missions, but scientists are still trying to make the science-fiction idea safe for humans  The Times of India
